title: training_anchor (v1)
description: A labeled instance (or labeled set) Ergon's calibration battery can verify against ground truth. Tier-3 mandatory. Trust-tier enum is load-bearing per Wave 3 of the 2026-05-10 batch (AA-019/020/017): every training_anchor MUST distinguish analytically_proven from numerically_certified from ml_predicted to prevent murmuration-style ground-truth contamination.

_schema_version.description: Per T-2026-05-11-aporia-to-techne-bs_coverage-schema-approved-as-v1.1: enum 1.0.0 or 1.1.0. v1.1.0 adds optional bs_coverage field; v1.0.0 entries continue to validate unchanged.

anchor_type.title: What kind of label this anchor carries

dataset_source.title: Where the labeled data lives
dataset_source.description: URL, LMFDB table name, paper reference, or repo path. Free-form but should be re-resolvable by a downstream consumer.

instance_count.title: Number of labeled instances
instance_count.description: Null acceptable when the set is implicitly defined (e.g. 'all hyperbolic knots <= 20 crossings' without enumerating the count).

prompt_template.title: Natural-language form of the verification question
prompt_template.description: Template for how an Ergon Learner-Tester would frame the verification question. Should include placeholders (e.g. {invariant_name}, {input_object}) so the same template applies across the full labeled set.

expected_answer_shape.title: Type description of the expected answer
expected_answer_shape.description: Free-form but should be parseable by Ergon as a Python type or schema. Examples: 'Tuple[int, int]', 'one of {trefoil, figure_eight, ...}', 'float in (0, 1)'.

verification_method.description: How each instance's label was established. Distinct from trust_tier (which is the substrate-grade epistemic strength).

trust_tier.description: Substrate-grade trust tier. Per Wave 3 2026-05-10 finding: distinguishing analytically_proven vs numerically_certified vs ml_predicted is LOAD-BEARING for downstream Learner training corpus. ml_predicted entries CANNOT be used as ground truth without explicit caveats (anti-murmuration discipline).

source.title: Primary citation for the dataset
source.description: Uses anyOf (not oneOf) because arXiv IDs and DOIs match both the structured pattern and the free-form fallback; downstream consumers examine the string directly. Same pattern fix as claim_v1.json (Day-1 catch).

anyOf.description: Free-form citation when no arXiv ID / DOI applies (e.g. 'LMFDB Modular Forms database, accessed 2026-05-11').

caveats.title: Measure-zero exceptions, AI-prediction admixture, completeness qualifiers
caveats.description: Free-form paragraph capturing all caveats the reviewer should know. Required even if 'no caveats known' — the substrate's epistemic-explicitness discipline says caveats not enumerated are caveats not considered.

consumed_by.title: Downstream consumer (Ergon script path or queue entry)

bs_coverage.title: Blind-spot coverage links
bs_coverage.description: List of BS-NNN blind-spot entry IDs this training_anchor covers. Per T-2026-05-11-aporia-to-techne-bs_coverage-schema-approved-as-v1.1 + Ergon's training_anchor_ingestion_spec.md §3.1 + §5.1 Gap 1: explicit blind-spot mapping replaces Ergon's regex-heuristic search of prompt_template/caveats/source/dataset_source. When this field is present (v1.1.0 emitters), Ergon's derive_bs_coverage prefers it over regex inference. Empty list and absent field are both legal; absent means consumer falls back to heuristic.

scale.coverage_qualifier: all prime knots <= 12 crossings, KnotInfo 2024-12 snapshot

examples.prompt_template: Compute the Alexander polynomial of the knot {knot_id} (given as a Gauss code or DT notation).
examples.expected_answer_shape: sympy.Poly over ZZ in variable t
examples.caveats: Coverage extends only to prime knots through 12 crossings; composite knots and higher-crossing-number knots are out of scope. Alexander polynomial is well-defined up to units (factor of +/- t^k); answers should be normalized to the canonical form per KnotInfo conventions.

examples.dataset_source: Cohen 1963 forcing construction; Kunen 'Set Theory' Ch. VII
examples.prompt_template: Who proved that the Continuum Hypothesis is independent of ZFC, and what year?
examples.caveats: Q-shaped anchor whose answer ('Cohen, 1963') lives in the source/dataset_source rather than the prompt_template. Demonstrates the bs_coverage field replacing Ergon's regex-heuristic search — explicit BS-001 link, no inference needed.
